expectations.text: Advises aiming tests where failures actually concentrate (the genuinely complex/failure-prone components), not assuming two paths behave the same without validating
expectations.evidence: Section '## 1. Aim tests where failures actually concentrate': 'Bugs cluster in complexity, and tests that don't exercise the genuinely failure-prone areas aren't really testing the system' plus a concrete failure-prone list (save-load, netcode, physics, pathfinding). The second half is explicit: '**Don't assume two code paths "do the same thing" without validating it.**' ... 'validating that assumption is the actual testing work.'

expectations.text: Treats test infrastructure as maintenance debt and applies a cost/benefit lens to the test suite itself
expectations.evidence: Section 2: 'Apply a cost/benefit lens **to the test infrastructure itself**, not just to the production code. A dedicated fast test mode is not free: it's one more thing to build, and then to maintain for the entire life of the project.' Also '"drowned by one-more-things" failure mode' and 'That's debt on top of debt.'

expectations.text: Presents Jonathan Blow's skepticism of testing orthodoxy as his stance while keeping the transferable kernel
expectations.evidence: Repeatedly attributed to Blow as his stance: 'Jonathan Blow's observation (his stance, but it generalizes)'; 'Blow's warning (again, his stance)'. Closing framing note: 'the skepticism above ... is Jonathan Blow's stance on testing orthodoxy, offered because the kernel transfers well. It's not an argument against testing; it's an argument for spending the test budget where it pays off' — explicitly preserves the transferable kernel.

expectations.text: Advises aiming tests where failures actually concentrate (the genuinely complex/failure-prone components), not assuming two paths behave the same without validating
expectations.evidence: Heading '## Aim tests where failures actually concentrate' + 'Concentrate on the genuinely complex, failure-prone systems, not UI glue or trivial accessors.' Second half explicit: '**Don't assume two paths behave the same without validating it.**' ... 'that agreement is a behavior to test, not an assumption to make.'

expectations.text: Treats test infrastructure as maintenance debt and applies a cost/benefit lens to the test suite itself
expectations.evidence: 'Treat the test suite — and any harness supporting it — as a maintenance commitment, not a free good. Apply a cost/benefit lens' and '**But it is debt.** More test machinery does not mean more confidence — it means more maintenance. A bespoke test mode is code you now own and must keep in sync; it can rot silently.'

expectations.text: Presents Jonathan Blow's skepticism of testing orthodoxy as his stance while keeping the transferable kernel
expectations.evidence: The answer never mentions Jonathan Blow anywhere. The transferable kernel (test where failures concentrate, infra-as-debt) is present, but the assertion requires presenting it AS Blow's stance / skepticism of testing orthodoxy, which is entirely absent. The transcript confirms: 'the baseline snapshot contains no Jonathan Blow framing, so none was added.' Framing is instead attributed to Kent Beck's desiderata (Fast/Writable/Predictive).

notes: Assertions 1-2 (aim tests where failures concentrate; test infra as maintenance debt) pass in BOTH configs - non-discriminating; baseline grounded them in Kent Beck desiderata.
Sole discriminator: A3 'present as Jonathan Blow's stance' - baseline never named Blow (attribution-only delta).
